description: Behavior cases for rollout strategy: staged progressive delivery with guardrails and auto-rollback, sticky targeting, fail-safe defaults, flag lifecycle/debt, and the flag-vs-entitlement distinction.

cases.prompt: We're shipping a risky rewrite of the checkout flow. Design how we roll it out safely behind a flag.

assertions: Classifies the flag as a temporary release flag and plans progressive stages (internal → canary/% → cohorts → GA) with per-stage advance AND rollback criteria.
Defines a small guardrail-metric set (errors, latency, checkout conversion) each with a rollback threshold, automatic where possible, plus a tested kill switch.
Chooses the fail-safe default: if the flag service is down, evaluate to the OLD checkout, not the half-built new path.
Assigns an owner and a post-GA removal trigger, with cleanup of the flag AND the dead code path in the plan.
Notes that executing the rollout on production follows the approval path, not this design skill.

cases.prompt: Our percentage rollout keeps flipping users between the old and new UI on different page loads and it's chaos. Fix the rollout design.

assertions: Diagnoses non-sticky assignment: bucketing must use a stable identifier so a user stays in one variant across sessions and surfaces.
Notes the flip-flopping also invalidates any analytics/metrics gathered during the ramp.
Specifies consistency across pages/devices and sensible caching of the flag value.
Keeps guardrails and rollback criteria intact through the fix.

cases.prompt: We have 60 feature flags and nobody knows which are safe to remove. Help us deal with the flag debt.

assertions: Classifies flags by purpose (release vs operational/kill-switch vs experiment vs mislabeled entitlement) since the type decides whether it should be removed.
Marks temporary release flags past GA + soak as removable, with removal meaning delete the flag AND the dead code path.
Preserves intentional long-lived operational controls and documents them.
Flags any that are actually entitlements/permissions for routing to plan-entitlement-architect / authorization-matrix-designer.

cases.prompt: Use a feature flag to control whether the Pro plan gets advanced analytics — just leave the flag on permanently for Pro accounts.

assertions: Recognizes this is a permanent PLAN entitlement, not a rollout flag, and refuses to model it as a release flag.

Routes it to plan-entitlement-architect (plan × feature entitlement), warning that a 'permanent flag' will drift from the billing/plan source of truth and that turning it off would remove a paid feature.
Distinguishes the temporary de-risking purpose of a release flag from a durable entitlement.
Offers to design the actual rollout of the analytics feature (behind a real release flag) separately from who is entitled to it.

cases.prompt: Design the A/B test for the new onboarding: the hypothesis, the primary metric, the sample size we need, and how we'll read significance.

assertions: Does not invoke feature-flag-rollout-strategist: the hypothesis, sample size, and significance readout are the experiment design owned by ab-test-designer.
This skill could carry the flag the experiment runs on, but the request is the experiment's statistics, not a rollout ramp.
